title: Rejoin-Clean-Air Traffic Surrogates
summary: F1 analysis notes that pit timing is heavily driven by whether a driver can stop into clean air and avoid losing time behind traffic.
rationale: F1 analysis notes that pit timing is heavily driven by whether a driver can stop into clean air and avoid losing time behind traffic. Even without explicit gap-to-car data, the table contains enough race-lap context to build a surrogate for rejoin quality.
implementation_hint: Estimate race-specific pit-loss proxies from RaceProgress/LapNumber context and use same-lap field order to approximate where a driver would rejoin if they stopped now. Engineer features such as estimated rejoin position band, nearby-field LapTime_Delta congestion, and clean-air flags when the likely rejoin zone has slow or volatile cars.
expected_effect: Should help on marginal decisions where tyre wear says 'maybe' but traffic makes the timing clearly good or bad.
risk: The proxy can be noisy because true time gaps are unobserved; gains depend on careful race-relative normalization.
